records.ety: [OE. trifle, trufle, OF. trufle mockery, raillery, trifle, probably the same word as F. truffe truffle, the word being applied to any small or worthless object. See Truffle.]

defs: A thing of very little value or importance; a paltry, or trivial, affair.
quotes: Trifles light as air Are to the jealous confirmation strong As proofs of holy writ.
Small sands the mountain, moments make year, And frifles life.

defs: A dish composed of sweetmeats, fruits, cake, wine, etc., with syllabub poured over it.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Trifled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Trifling (?).]

defs: To act or talk without seriousness, gravity, weight, or dignity; to act or talk with levity; to indulge in light or trivial amusements.
quotes: They trifle, and they beat the air about nothing which toucheth us.

defs: To make of no importance; to treat as a trifle.
To spend in vanity; to fritter away; to waste; as, to trifle away money.
